response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically accomplished senior engineer with strong open-source credentials and broad full-stack experience, but falls critically short on the two most non-negotiable requirements for this role: German language proficiency (self-reported as 'Basic' against a stated C1 minimum for a client-facing German consulting firm) and demonstrated leadership experience (the resume shows a consistent individual-contributor track record with no evidence of managing teams, owning project delivery end-to-end, or advising clients on architecture in a consulting capacity). The GISCollective founder role is the closest signal, but it is an open-source side project rather than managed commercial delivery. UML, formal requirements management, and IT risk/compliance experience are also absent from the resume.
strengths: Deep, polyglot engineering background spanning 15+ years across embedded, web, mobile, and distributed systems with consistent quality signals (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based dev)
Genuine open-source commitment demonstrated through GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial, and public conference talks (FOSS4G 2019)
Linux and DevOps familiarity including GitLab administration, Docker, Kubernetes, and CI infrastructure setup at Altom
concerns: German proficiency listed as 'Basic' — the role explicitly requires C1 minimum for client advisory work in a German-market consulting firm; this is likely a hard blocker
No evidence of people management, team leadership, or client-facing consulting delivery; all roles are individual-contributor positions with no direct reports or P&L responsibility
No mention of UML, formal IT architecture frameworks, requirements management methodologies, or IT security/risk/compliance work — all listed as required qualifications

key_factors.factor: No team leadership or management experience
